growing_areas: Any Climatic Zone Except Upcountry Wet Zone
soil_types: Well-Drained Soil That Is Neither Acidic Nor Alkaline

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Spreads Okra Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus.
pests.solution: Vector control using insect-resistant nets, cover crops, or neem extract.

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: White powdery fungal growth on the underside of leaves, yellowing of upper leaf surface, primarily affects mature leaves and stems. Leaves eventually dry and fall, leading to significant yield reduction. Disease severity increases with high humidity and dew.
diseases.solution: Maintain clean cultivation, control micro-humidity and temperature, use recommended fungicides in severe cases or under favorable climatic conditions.

diseases.name: Okra Yellow Vein Mosaic Virus
diseases.impact: Characteristic yellowing of leaf veins, reduced yield, yellow mosaic patterns on leaves, leaves curl, wrinkle, and develop spots. Stunted plant growth. Severe infection leads to small, deformed, yellowed, and shriveled pods. Not seed-borne.
diseases.solution: Control the Whitefly vector (using insect-resistant nets, cover crops, neem extract). Practice field sanitation, remove and destroy diseased plants immediately. Use recommended healthy seeds and resistant varieties. Exercise caution when using tools. Implement Integrated Pest Management (IPM) strategies, including crop rotation with non-host plants, maintaining proper planting distance, ensuring proper plant nutrition, good water management, keeping the field clean, and utilizing biological control methods.
